Dashboard Archive: 2026-04-01 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Long-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns from 1 month to 10 years, with relative performance vs SPY over 1y/5y/10y. Same scoring engine as the short-term ranker but optimized for longer investment horizons and secular trends.
Today's Reading
LINC ranks #1 with an exceptional 95.1 score, driven by explosive long-term returns (1,501.57% over 10 years, 500.89% over 5 years) that significantly outpace SPY across all timeframes (+1,283.6% vs SPY over 10 years). The stock shows textbook momentum with all four moving average benchmarks above price, a "strong_buy" signal, and clean momentum readings, though its 1.515 volatility ratio suggests elevated risk relative to recent price movement. CLMT ranks #2 with strong near-term performance (172.57% over 1 year) but a critical structural warning: its 10-year return of 165.31% actually underperformed SPY by 52.66 points, suggesting the bulk of gains are recent rather than part of a sustained secular trend. Both stocks pass all moving average filters and show materials sector correlation (XLB), but LINC's consistent outperformance across 1y/5y/10y windows contrasts sharply with CLMT's recent strength masking longer-term underperformance.
For educational and informational purposes only. Not financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results.

Click column headers to sort  |  Score = percentile composite: returns + ratio passes + bad-SPY resilience  |  SMA ✓ = price above all SMAs (30/50/100/200)  |  Ratio = short-term momentum consistency (out of 5 tests)  |  M = momentum signal (sortable) —  ● clean  ● stalling  ● overextended  |  V = volume confirmation (sortable) —  ● confirmed  ● normal  ○ thin  |  PB = entry timing: down day on strong stock (sortable by 1d return) —  ● buy today  ○ none  |  OL = theme overlap vs owned tickers —  ● overlap  ○ clear
